2026-05-22.title: The Nebulous Realm of WR 134
2026-05-22.explanation: This sharp telescopic portrait captures the glowing edge of a ring-like nebula traced by ionized hydrogen and oxygen gas. The shells of material were swept up by the fierce stellar winds of WR 134, a rare and luminous Wolf-Rayet star roughly 6,000 light-years away in the constellation Cygnus. Such stars are massive and short-lived, shedding their outer layers before ending as supernovae.

2026-05-24.title: A Martian Eclipse: Phobos Crosses the Sun
2026-05-24.explanation: Captured by the Perseverance rover's Mastcam-Z camera in 2022, this video shows the Martian moon Phobos passing in front of the Sun. The whole transit lasted only about 40 seconds, far quicker than a solar eclipse on Earth, because Phobos orbits Mars closely and rapidly. Tracking such eclipses helps scientists refine the moon's orbit and study how it slowly spirals toward the planet.

2026-05-25.title: Thackeray's Globules
2026-05-25.explanation: These opaque clouds of interstellar dust and gas are silhouetted against the bright stellar nursery IC 2944, about 7,600 light-years away in the constellation Centaurus. Known as Thackeray's Globules, the dense clumps are being fractured and eroded by the intense ultraviolet radiation of nearby young, hot stars. They may be too small to ever collapse and form stars of their own.

2026-05-27.title: PK 164 +31.1: The Headphone Nebula
2026-05-27.explanation: This faint planetary nebula, the remnant of a dying Sun-like star, spans a region of the constellation Lynx about one-fifth the apparent diameter of the full moon. Its headphone-like shape arises where hydrogen lobes pierce an oxygen-rich inner region, a geometry that hints a stellar or planetary companion helped shape the nebula's formation.

2026-06-02.title: The Vela Supernova Remnant
2026-06-02.explanation: About twelve thousand years ago a star in the southern constellation Vela exploded, briefly creating a new point of light in the skies of early humans. Today the expanding debris forms the Vela Supernova Remnant, a tangle of glowing filaments spanning some 100 light-years. The blast also left behind a rapidly spinning neutron star, the Vela pulsar.

2026-06-05.title: The Hydra Cluster of Galaxies
2026-06-05.explanation: While the spiky foreground stars lie only hundreds of light-years away, the galaxies of the Hydra Cluster are well over 100 million light-years distant. Three dominant galaxies anchor the cluster near its center, among hundreds of members bound together by gravity in one of the nearer large clusters of galaxies.

2026-06-07.title: Jupiter and Venus from Earth
2026-06-07.explanation: A close conjunction of Jupiter and Venus, the two brightest planets in the night sky, drew the two worlds to within about three degrees of each other. This evening view captures the pair gleaming low over the horizon from Poland, a celestial pairing visible to skywatchers across much of the Earth.

2026-06-09.title: Thor's Helmet
2026-06-09.explanation: Thor's Helmet is a hat-shaped cosmic cloud about 30 light-years across, in fact an interstellar bubble blown by the fast wind of a hot, massive Wolf-Rayet star near its center. Cataloged as NGC 2359, the nebula lies around 12,000 light-years away in the constellation Canis Major, its filaments shaped by interaction with a nearby molecular cloud.
